title: Hypertension (High Blood Pressure)
root_mechanism: Sympathetic nervous system overactivation + vascular endothelial inflammation + renin–angiotensin–aldosterone system (RAAS) dysregulation → sustained vasoconstriction and sodium retention → elevated systemic vascular resistance → elevated BP.

interventions.intervention: Slow deep breathing (4-7-8 technique) + sunlight (10 min)
interventions.physiological_effect: 4-7-8 breathing activates vagus nerve → reduces sympathetic outflow → lowers peripheral vascular resistance within minutes

interventions.intervention: Cold water spinal pack (cervical to lumbar)
interventions.physiological_effect: Cold stimulates posterior hypothalamus → paradoxically resets autonomic balance; reduces adrenal catecholamine surge

interventions.intervention: Abhyanga (warm sesame oil) head + foot massage
interventions.physiological_effect: Head massage stimulates baroreceptors in scalp vessels; foot massage activates reflexology zones linked to kidney/heart → lowers RAAS activity

interventions.intervention: Nadi Shodhana + Bhramari pranayama (humming bee breath)
interventions.physiological_effect: Bhramari produces nitric oxide in nasal sinuses → diffuses into pulmonary circulation → systemic vasodilation

interventions.intervention: Breakfast: DASH diet — oats, banana, flaxseed, low-fat milk
interventions.physiological_effect: Potassium (banana) competes with sodium at renal tubule → natriuresis; flaxseed omega-3 reduces vascular inflammation

interventions.intervention: Cold abdominal pack
interventions.physiological_effect: Reduces splanchnic blood pooling; lowers cardiac preload; mild vagal activation

interventions.intervention: Beetroot juice (200 mL) or Arjuna bark decoction
interventions.physiological_effect: Dietary nitrates → nitric oxide via salivary bacteria → smooth muscle relaxation → direct vasodilation

interventions.intervention: Low-sodium meal: whole grains + leafy greens + legumes + curd
interventions.physiological_effect: Magnesium in greens relaxes vascular smooth muscle; calcium in curd modulates intracellular calcium balance

interventions.intervention: Yoga Nidra or 20-min rest in Savasana
interventions.physiological_effect: Induces theta brainwave state → cortisol drops 30–40% → reduces renal angiotensin release

interventions.intervention: Yoga sequence (see below)
interventions.physiological_effect: Inversions improve baroreceptor sensitivity; forward bends reduce cardiac workload

interventions.intervention: Warm mustard oil foot bath + contrast arm bath
interventions.physiological_effect: Peripheral vasodilation shifts blood to periphery → reduces central pressure; mustard oil allyl isothiocyanate relaxes arterioles

interventions.intervention: Light dinner: soup + steamed veg + small grain portion; no salt after 5 PM
interventions.physiological_effect: Eliminating evening sodium prevents nocturnal BP surge; light meal reduces cardiac demand during sleep

interventions.intervention: Warm full-body Epsom salt bath
interventions.physiological_effect: Transdermal magnesium → smooth muscle relaxation; warmth lowers systemic vascular resistance

interventions.intervention: Progressive muscle relaxation or guided meditation
interventions.physiological_effect: Reduces muscle tension-mediated sympathetic feedforward; lowers overnight cortisol

interventions.intervention: Cold spinal pack
Abhyanga (head + foot)
Epsom salt bath
Contrast arm/foot bath
Yoga + pranayama
DASH diet
Beetroot/Arjuna decoction

relapse_rules.action: Return to Phase 2 for 2 weeks + daily spinal pack

fasting_rules.physiological_effect: Overnight fast lowers nocturnal insulin → reduces aldosterone-driven sodium retention → morning BP lower
Prevents nocturnal BP surge driven by evening sodium load and RAAS overnight activation
Reduces extracellular fluid volume → lowers cardiac preload and vascular resistance

fasting_rules.specification: Banana or leafy greens at breakfast
fasting_rules.physiological_effect: Dietary potassium competes with sodium at renal tubule → natriuresis → reduces blood volume

fasting_rules.specification: 200 mL / one cup — 15 min before lunch
fasting_rules.physiological_effect: Dietary nitrates → NO via salivary bacteria → smooth muscle relaxation → acute vasodilation reducing SBP 4–8 mmHg

phase_macros.carbohydrates: Moderate-low: 40–45% of calories (low-GI, high potassium sources only)
phase_macros.protein: Moderate: 20–25% of calories (plant-dominant: legumes, curd, fish)
phase_macros.fat: Moderate: 30–35% (omega-3 emphasis — flaxseed, fish, walnuts; strictly no saturated fats)
phase_macros.rationale: DASH-aligned macros with strict sodium restriction; potassium and magnesium maximised; saturated fat minimised to reduce vascular stiffness

phase_macros.carbohydrates: Moderate: 45–50% of calories (introduce more whole grains and fruit)
phase_macros.rationale: Sustained DASH pattern; increasing dietary diversity while maintaining sodium < 1500 mg/day and potassium > 3500 mg/day
Long-term cardiovascular protective diet; sodium < 2000 mg/day lifelong; processed food elimination maintained

items: Hibiscus tea (2 cups/day — ACE inhibitor effect)

meals.what_to_eat: 2 glasses warm water + 2 raw garlic cloves
meals.physiological_rationale: Garlic allicin inhibits ACE enzyme → reduces angiotensin II → vasodilation; warm water initiates peristalsis

meals.what_to_eat: Hibiscus tea (1 cup, no sugar) or beetroot juice (100 mL)
meals.physiological_rationale: Hibiscus anthocyanins inhibit ACE; beetroot nitrates convert to NO in salivary glands → acute vasodilation

meals.what_to_eat: Oats porridge (no salt, no sugar) + 1 banana + ground flaxseed 1 tbsp + low-fat milk or curd
meals.physiological_rationale: Oats beta-glucan reduces vascular inflammation; banana potassium promotes natriuresis; flaxseed ALA reduces systolic BP 3–5 mmHg

meals.what_to_eat: Pomegranate (½ fruit) or 4–5 walnuts + hibiscus tea
meals.physiological_rationale: Pomegranate punicalagins: ACE inhibition + antioxidant protection of vascular endothelium; walnuts provide vascular-protective omega-3

meals.what_to_eat: Beetroot juice 200 mL or Arjuna bark decoction — 15 min before eating
meals.physiological_rationale: Pre-meal NO boost; reduces postprandial BP spike which is common in hypertensives

meals.what_to_eat: Brown rice (small portion, no added salt) + dal (no salt or minimal) + spinach sabzi + curd (unsalted) + salad (cucumber, tomato)
meals.physiological_rationale: High potassium/magnesium meal; legumes provide arginine (NO precursor); curd calcium modulates vascular smooth muscle tone

meals.what_to_eat: Celery sticks + 1 cup green tea (no sugar)
meals.physiological_rationale: Celery phthalides relax arterial smooth muscle; green tea EGCG improves endothelial function

meals.what_to_eat: Moong dal soup + steamed vegetables (broccoli, spinach) + small ragi roti (no salt) — NO sodium after this
meals.physiological_rationale: Light low-sodium dinner prevents nocturnal aldosterone activation; ragi high in magnesium for vascular relaxation

meals.what_to_eat: Warm water or herbal tea only (tulsi, cinnamon) — no food
meals.physiological_rationale: 12-hour overnight fast reduces overnight insulin and aldosterone → morning BP reduction

functional_foods.name: Beetroot juice
functional_foods.dose_timing: 200 mL fresh juice — 15 min before lunch, daily
functional_foods.mechanism_of_action: Dietary nitrates → nitric oxide via salivary bacterial nitrate reductase → smooth muscle relaxation → reduces SBP 4–10 mmHg within 3 hours

functional_foods.name: Garlic (raw)
functional_foods.dose_timing: 2 raw cloves daily — crushed and left 10 min before eating (morning)
functional_foods.mechanism_of_action: Allicin inhibits ACE enzyme → reduces angiotensin II → vasodilation; hydrogen sulfide production relaxes vascular smooth muscle

functional_foods.name: Arjuna bark (Terminalia arjuna)
functional_foods.dose_timing: 1 cup decoction (5 g bark boiled in 300 mL water) — daily before lunch
functional_foods.mechanism_of_action: Arjunolic acid and glycosides have ACE-inhibiting, cardioprotective, and beta-blocking properties; improves cardiac output

functional_foods.name: Hibiscus tea
functional_foods.dose_timing: 2 cups/day (morning and afternoon) — steep 5 min, no sugar
functional_foods.mechanism_of_action: Anthocyanins and quercetin inhibit ACE; documented to reduce SBP 7 mmHg and DBP 3 mmHg in 6 weeks (RCT data)

functional_foods.name: Flaxseeds (ground)
functional_foods.dose_timing: 2 tbsp freshly ground — added to breakfast daily
functional_foods.mechanism_of_action: ALA omega-3 reduces endothelin-1 (vasoconstrictor); lignans reduce vascular inflammation; reduces SBP 3–5 mmHg in hypertensives

functional_foods.name: Celery (Ajwain/Shalari)
functional_foods.dose_timing: 4 stalks raw daily or celery seed water (1 tsp seeds in 500 mL)
functional_foods.mechanism_of_action: 3-n-Butylphthalide (3nB) relaxes arterial smooth muscle; diuretic effect reduces blood volume; inhibits RAAS locally

functional_foods.name: Coriander seed water
functional_foods.dose_timing: 1 tsp coriander seeds soaked overnight — drink water in morning
functional_foods.mechanism_of_action: Acts as mild ACE inhibitor; diuretic effect promotes natriuresis; anti-inflammatory on vascular endothelium

functional_foods.name: Pomegranate juice
functional_foods.dose_timing: 100–150 mL fresh pomegranate juice — daily morning
functional_foods.mechanism_of_action: Punicalagins are the most potent ACE inhibitors in plant kingdom; reduces arterial stiffness; anti-atherosclerotic via LDL oxidation inhibition

asanas.physiological_effect: Nitric oxide production in nasal sinuses → pulmonary vasodilation; vagal activation reduces sympathetic outflow
Reduces cardiac sympathetic tone; gentle aortic stretch; parasympathetic shift reduces HR and BP
Baroreceptor reset via increased venous return; gravity-assisted reduction of peripheral resistance; reduces preload
Mild inversion; improves baroreceptor sensitivity at carotid sinus; strengthens parasympathetic regulation
Opens chest cavity; increases HRV via vagal activation; reduces anxiety-driven sympathetic surges
Gentle abdominal compression reduces splanchnic pooling; strong parasympathetic shift
Sustained nitric oxide production; HRV improvement; reduces cortisol and vascular catecholamines
Full parasympathetic dominance; cortisol normalisation; acute SBP reduction 5–10 mmHg documented post-Savasana

red_flags.action: Stop all stimulating interventions; urgent hospital referral immediately
Rule out hypertensive encephalopathy or stroke — emergency referral

red_flags.sign: Chest pain or shortness of breath
red_flags.action: Rule out hypertensive heart disease; urgent ECG

red_flags.sign: Sudden confusion, facial droop, arm weakness
red_flags.action: Stroke protocol — call emergency services immediately
Hypotension — hold all naturopathic vasodilating treatments; reduce drug dose with physician

clinical_disclaimer: This protocol is designed to be implemented under the supervision of a qualified BNYS physician. Patients on antihypertensive medications must have drug doses reviewed before initiating any vasodilating naturopathic interventions. Do not abruptly discontinue antihypertensives. This document is for clinical reference only and does not substitute for individualised medical assessment.
